refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall minimize energy consumption per ton moved to meet or exceed the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the high-level FR into a measurable and actionable sub-FR focused on energy consumption per ton moved aligns with the acceptance criteria and enables targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure total energy used and tons moved over the evaluation period and verify that energy_per_ton ≤ E_target.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor and report energy consumption accurately.
FR2: Optimize vehicle operation to reduce energy per ton moved.
FR3: Provide feedback and control to maintain energy efficiency target.

candidate_dps: DP1: Integrate energy metering sensors and telemetry for accurate energy data capture.
DP2: Implement vehicle operational modes and control algorithms to optimize energy use.
DP3: Develop a feedback control system to adjust operations based on energy efficiency KPIs.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Integrate energy metering sensors and telemetry for accurate energy data capture.

potential_sources: Telemetry data shared between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules
Control feedback loops involving Safety Services
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically supports FR1 and interfaces well with other DPs; coupling is manageable with clear interface definitions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes energy metering sensors provide reliable and accurate data.
Risk of data latency affecting real-time control decisions.
Safety criticality requires robust fail-safe mechanisms in energy monitoring.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP2 and DP3 in subsequent decomposition levels to address optimization and feedback control.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical components including energy metering sensors and onboard telemetry systems responsible for energy data acquisition.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Energy consumption data transmitted to NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication channel for data exchange and control commands.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as energy metering is physically embedded; low diffusion risk.
Moderate containment; some data processing but less direct control over sensors.
Low containment; primarily receives telemetry data, higher diffusion risk if duplicated.
Minimal containment; may receive alerts but not responsible for energy data acquisition.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy metering and telemetry are best contained within the Vehicle module to minimize diffusion and ensure data integrity.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules.
Risk of sensor failure impacting energy efficiency monitoring.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts and redundancy for sensor data to mitigate risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (energy metering and telemetry integration) directly addresses the refined FR and supports measurable verification.

diffusion_risks: Possible duplication of telemetry processing in Teleoperation if not well modularized.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Assigning DP1 to Vehicle module ensures strong containment of physical sensing and data acquisition.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data collection and energy consumption measurement is feasible within Vehicle and NGHE Solution modules.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of energy metering to Vehicle and data consumption to NGHE Solution and Teleoperation.

recommended_revisions: Plan subsequent decomposition for optimization and feedback control DPs.
Strengthen interface definitions to reduce diffusion risks.

decision.justification: While the initial DP and IM assignment are sound, the FR refinement can be further decomposed to cover optimization and feedback control aspects for a complete solution.
next_level_sub_frs: FR2: Optimize vehicle operation to reduce energy per ton moved.
FR3: Provide feedback and control to maintain energy efficiency target.

revise.why: To decompose the energy efficiency KPI into actionable sub-FRs enabling targeted DP and IM assignments.

assumptions: Energy metering sensors are accurate and reliable.
Telemetry interfaces are stable and secure.
open_risks: Sensor failure or data latency impacting energy efficiency monitoring.
Interface complexity leading to diffusion of energy data processing.
evidence_needed: Sensor accuracy and reliability test results.
